evidence: Four specialist write agents hit authorization limits while targeting repositories outside the writable workspace.
cause: Write delegation happened before validating subagent filesystem permissions.
remediation: Pilot one agent after writable-scope preflight; keep external-workspace agents read-only and apply changes from the principal agent.

evidence: Eleven wait calls exhausted configured budgets totaling approximately 1510 seconds.
cause: Workers were polled without a strict progress and takeover gate.
remediation: Wait once for at most 60 seconds, inspect progress, and take over after two no-progress observations.

evidence: Three workers were manually closed or taken over after prolonged silence.
cause: Delegated writes could not complete in their permission scope.
remediation: Delegate read-only audits outside writable roots and reserve edits for writable worktrees.

evidence: The 100000-message benchmark timed out without producing a result.
remediation: Run a one-percent benchmark calibration and estimate the full timeout before the production workload.

evidence: A late default change forced another full build and an unchanged benchmark was repeated.
cause: Defaults were finalized after expensive validation and default wiring was verified with a full run.
remediation: Finalize defaults before Docker and verify argument wiring with a tiny smoke workload.

evidence: Two apply_patch calls failed under the Windows sandbox.
cause: The patch tool could not enforce the configured split writable roots.
remediation: Stage inside one writable worktree and use one supported patch path before starting broad edits.

evidence: Exact replacement, interpolation, newline, path API, parser, and staging-path mistakes required repeat work.
cause: One-off PowerShell diagnostics were not syntax-checked or tested on a single repository first.
remediation: Syntax-check generated PowerShell and pilot every inventory transformation on one repository.

evidence: Forty-five commits were authored on the excluded date; the user attributed that work to Antigravity and OpenCode.
remediation: Preserve this event for provenance and exclude it from Codex efficiency totals.

evidence: Three apply_patch attempts failed because the Windows sandbox rejected split writable roots.
cause: The current patch tool cannot prepare the configured Windows sandbox.
remediation: Use a staged single-root edit path and do not retry the same failing tool after the first confirmed platform failure.

evidence: The first 30-repository credential scan exceeded a 10-second command timeout; the retry completed.
cause: The timeout did not account for thirty Git process startups.
remediation: Budget repository-wide Git scans from a one-repository timing sample.

evidence: Two malformed git patches, one unterminated PowerShell edit command, and one unhandled native Git stderr path required correction.
cause: Fallback editing and native-command handling were not piloted with the smallest possible change.
remediation: Pilot one minimal edit and one failing native Git call before applying a workspace-wide script.

evidence: Portfolio report exceeded 60 seconds because validation spawned repeated Git processes for each evidence type.
cause: Tracked-file inventory was queried repeatedly instead of cached once per repository.
remediation: Use one git ls-files and one porcelain-v2 status snapshot per repository, then filter in memory.

evidence: The first cached but serial Git inventory still exceeded a 30-second timeout.
remediation: Collect read-only Git snapshots through a bounded cross-version runspace pool.

evidence: Two invalid Measure-Command invocations and one PowerShell-7-only parallel implementation were rejected.
cause: Runtime compatibility and command shape were not checked before timing.
remediation: Read PSVersion first and use Stopwatch plus a PowerShell-5.1-compatible runspace pool.
